metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete in-session assertion to the time-to-land AC that checks the PutMetricData payload uses `Value` not `StatisticValues` — e.g. assert `mock_put_metric_data.call_args` contains `{'Value': <float>}` and not `StatisticValues`.
findings.finding: The time-to-land 'in-session proof' ('the datapoint is published for one landed change') names no concrete proving command — an agent cannot verify the `Value` (not `StatisticValues`) shape of the PutMetricData call in-session.
findings.location: AC: Time-to-land metric
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Tests AC: 'In the unit test, assert `put_metric_data` was called with `MetricData[0]["Value"]` (a float) and that `MetricData[0]` has no `StatisticValues` key.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit PutMetricData call (in loop.py each poll cycle, or in observability.sh reading the status endpoint) that publishes a named `time_in_phase_seconds` metric (with a stated namespace and dimension strategy), so the CloudWatch alarm in `monitoring_autolander.tf` has a real metric to watch.
findings.finding: The stuck-state alarm has a broken data-flow: CloudWatch alarms watch metrics, not HTTP endpoint JSON. The plan exposes time-in-phase in the status endpoint but names no PutMetricData call (from loop.py or observability.sh) that would publish a watchable time_in_phase_seconds metric. Without a metric publisher, monitoring_autolander.tf cannot define a functional alarm expression, and the unit-check AC cannot validate a real CloudWatch metric query.
findings.location: AC: Stuck-state alarm (high visibility) / Files: infra/terraform/monitoring_autolander.tf + infra/autolander/loop.py
findings.suggested_fix: In loop.py, emit `aws cloudwatch put-metric-data` (or equivalent boto3 call) for `rebar/host:autolander_time_in_phase_seconds` (dimensionless, following the established pattern) on each poll cycle when wipChain is non-idle; the alarm in monitoring_autolander.tf then watches this metric with `GreaterThanThreshold` at the chosen seconds threshold.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the structured stdout marker the loop writes on each handback/lander_down event (e.g. `AUTOLANDER_HANDBACK`) and add it to the observability.sh feeder spec alongside `AUTOLANDER_ERROR`, so the third error feeder has a grounded grep target.
findings.finding: The `lander_down`/handback event feeder for `autolander_errors` has no grounded emission mechanism. `lander_down` is a property evaluated by the `land` client reading the status endpoint — it is not an event the bot emits. For observability.sh to count handback events as error datapoints, the bot must write a greppable structured marker (e.g. `AUTOLANDER_HANDBACK`) to stdout on each handback. The plan names only `AUTOLANDER_ERROR` as the structured marker; no handback marker is specified, leaving this feeder unimplementable as described.
findings.location: AC: Error metric (LOUD by default) — third feeder: 'each lander_down/handback event'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the missing data-flow for the stuck-state alarm: either add a `autolander_time_in_phase_seconds` (or equivalent) `PutMetricData` feeder in `observability.sh` (reading from the status endpoint or from `loop.py` stdout), or clarify that `loop.py` itself publishes this metric directly — whichever approach is chosen must be named in the Files/AC so the Terraform alarm has a real metric to watch.
findings.finding: The stuck-state alarm's data-flow is underspecified: the plan exposes `time-in-phase` in the HTTP status endpoint JSON but names no `PutMetricData` call that publishes it as a CloudWatch metric, yet CloudWatch alarms can only watch CloudWatch metrics — not HTTP endpoint fields. The `observability.sh` feeder list covers only `autolander_errors` and `autolander_time_to_land_seconds`; a time-in-phase metric feeder is absent.
findings.location: AC: Stuck-state alarm (high visibility); Files: infra/scripts/observability.sh
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the observability.sh EDIT spec: 'also polls the autolander status endpoint for time-in-phase and publishes `autolander_time_in_phase_seconds` (dimensionless) via PutMetricData; monitoring_autolander.tf watches this metric for the stuck-state alarm.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a Tests AC entry asserting that a simulated land emits exactly one `autolander_time_to_land_seconds` datapoint using the `Value` field (not `StatisticValues`), verifiable via a captured PutMetricData call.
findings.finding: The time-to-land metric ('emit autolander_time_to_land_seconds … individual Value datapoints per landed change') is specified as an in-session deliverable ('in-session proof = the datapoint is published'), but no corresponding test for this metric appears in the Tests AC bullet.
findings.location: AC line — Time-to-land metric AC; AC line — Tests bullet (omits time-to-land)
findings.suggested_fix: Mock the CloudWatch PutMetricData call in the test and assert: (a) the metric name is 'autolander_time_to_land_seconds', (b) the payload uses 'Value' not 'StatisticValues', and (c) the value is a positive number matching the elapsed time.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the concrete mechanism by which observability.sh detects `lander_down`/handback events for the third `autolander_errors` feeder (e.g., a named stdout marker `AUTOLANDER_LANDER_DOWN` written by loop.py that observability.sh greps from the autolander container's journald, following the VOTER_ERROR/AUTODEPLOY_ERROR pattern).
findings.finding: The plan names 'each `lander_down`/handback event' as one of three feeders for `autolander_errors` in observability.sh, but specifies no mechanism for how observability.sh detects these events — no journald grep pattern, no stdout marker, no endpoint poll, no event source is named. This is a new integration surface with an unspecified technical approach.
findings.location: AC: Error metric (LOUD by default) — 'each `lander_down`/handback event'
findings.suggested_fix: Follow the established pattern: have loop.py write a structured marker (e.g., `AUTOLANDER_HANDBACK`) to stdout on each lander_down/handback event, and have observability.sh grep the autolander container's journald (via `journalctl CONTAINER_NAME=compose-autolander-1`) for that marker using the same offset-delta pattern as sections 4/4c/4d.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the observable form of `lander_down`/handback events — e.g., specify that `loop.py` emits a structured `AUTOLANDER_HANDBACK` stdout marker (or reuses `AUTOLANDER_ERROR`) on each handback, and that `observability.sh` greps for it using the same journald offset-delta pattern as the other feeders.
findings.finding: The `lander_down`/handback feeder's counter-increment placement is ambiguous. The error metric AC lists 'each `lander_down`/handback event' as the third source for `autolander_errors`, but the plan does not specify whether `loop.py` emits a structured marker for these events (like `AUTOLANDER_ERROR`) that `observability.sh` can grep from journald, or whether `observability.sh` polls the status endpoint for a counter, or something else. Without knowing how the signal surfaces from the bot, the feeder cannot be implemented.
findings.location: AC: Error metric (LOUD by default) — third feeder description

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the loop.py→observability.sh split for `autolander_time_to_land_seconds`: either have loop.py call `PutMetricData` directly at land-time (eliminating observability.sh's role for this metric), or specify an explicit bridging contract (stdout log format + offset-file scraping scheme in observability.sh) that preserves every per-event datapoint.
findings.finding: The per-event `autolander_time_to_land_seconds` metric has a split-publish architecture that risks data loss: loop.py is designated the emit site but observability.sh is designated the PutMetricData feeder. A time-to-land value is produced exactly once per landed change (an instantaneous event), not at a fixed cadence. If observability.sh runs on a timer (e.g. every 5 minutes like the other probes), any landed change whose emit occurs between two probe runs can only be recovered if observability.sh parses loop.py's stdout for those events — but the plan never specifies this log-scraping mechanism, an offset file, or any bridging contract between loop.py and observability.sh for this per-event value.
findings.location: AC: Time-to-land metric / Files: infra/autolander/loop.py + infra/scripts/observability.sh
findings.suggested_fix: Have loop.py call PutMetricData directly (via boto3 or subprocess aws CLI) immediately when a change lands, with Value = elapsed seconds. Remove observability.sh's role for this metric. This is consistent with how the loop already has direct knowledge of when a change lands and matches the 'in-session proof = the datapoint is published' AC.

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend the status endpoint AC to specify the JSON response for at least three non-happy-path states: (1) loop initializing / no wipChain yet, (2) idle (no active changes), and (3) internal serialization error — including the HTTP status code returned in each case.
findings.finding: The status endpoint AC specifies only the happy-path JSON shape. It does not specify what the endpoint returns during loop initialization (before any `wipChain` exists), when the chain is idle/empty, or when an internal error prevents serialization — leaving `land` and agents without a defined contract for non-happy-path states.
findings.location: AC: Read-only status endpoint — 'serving JSON — the current `wipChain` … the count of `Autosubmit`-set-and-waiting changes, and `heartbeat_age_s`'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the default stuck-state threshold value in the AC (e.g. '30 minutes') so the agent can act without guessing.
findings.finding: The stuck-state alarm threshold ('past a threshold') is never stated — an agent must invent the value.
findings.location: AC: Stuck-state alarm (high visibility)
findings.suggested_fix: Add: 'threshold = 30 minutes (defaultable; override via Terraform variable `autolander_stuck_phase_threshold_minutes`)' to the stuck-state alarm AC.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a default port and bind address to the status endpoint AC (e.g. 'listen on 0.0.0.0:8080, configurable via `AUTOLANDER_STATUS_PORT`').
findings.finding: The read-only HTTP status endpoint has no stated port or bind address — an agent cannot wire it without guessing.
findings.location: AC: Read-only status endpoint
findings.suggested_fix: Append to the AC: 'Serve on `0.0.0.0:${AUTOLANDER_STATUS_PORT:-8080}`; document the port in the module README.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add the autolander Docker Compose container name (e.g., `AUTOLANDER_CONTAINER=compose-autolander-1`) to the observability.sh edit spec, cross-referencing the compose service name defined in the container setup ticket, so the journald feeder can be written correctly.
findings.finding: The autolander container name is never specified anywhere in the plan or codebase, but `observability.sh`'s feeder for the `AUTOLANDER_ERROR` stdout marker requires it (following the established `VOTER_CONTAINER=compose-review-bot-1` pattern). An implementer must invent the Docker Compose service/container name.
findings.location: AC: Error metric (LOUD by default) — AUTOLANDER_ERROR feeder; Files: infra/scripts/observability.sh
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the observability.sh EDIT spec: 'uses AUTOLANDER_CONTAINER=${AUTOLANDER_CONTAINER:-compose-autolander-1} (matching the service name in docker-compose.yml defined in [sibling ticket id]) for the journald AUTOLANDER_ERROR grep.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the threading/isolation model for the HTTP status endpoint in loop.py (e.g., 'run in a daemon thread; handler exceptions are caught and logged, never propagated to the main loop thread; shared state accessed under a read lock').
findings.finding: The plan adds an HTTP server inside loop.py but specifies no threading/concurrency model or failure-isolation boundary between the HTTP endpoint and the main autolander loop. If the HTTP server runs on the main thread (or shares its event loop) and raises an unhandled exception, it can interrupt or wedge the loop it is meant to observe.
findings.location: infra/autolander/loop.py — AC: Read-only status endpoint
findings.suggested_fix: Run the HTTP server in a `threading.Thread(daemon=True)` started before the main loop; wrap the request handler in a broad try/except that returns HTTP 500 on error without re-raising; access wipChain state via a lock or a snapshot copy.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the status endpoint's binding address and port (e.g., `127.0.0.1:<port>`, loopback-only) and provide an explicit, justified no-auth rationale (loopback-only / behind an authenticating gateway / private SG-only) or name a human/admin auth mechanism — the current 'bound so agents can reach it' is insufficient for T10(b2).
findings.finding: The plan stands up a new HTTP status endpoint on the autolander bot (loop.py) but never specifies the binding address, port, or how human/admin principals authenticate to it — the AC says only 'Read-only, non-secret; bound so agents can reach it', which is a partial no-auth claim but not an explicit justified rationale (loopback-only / behind an authenticating gateway / private network).
findings.location: AC: Read-only status endpoint — 'bound so agents can reach it'
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence to the AC: 'The endpoint binds to `127.0.0.1:<port>` (loopback-only on the EC2 host); access by agents is via SSM port-forwarding or the compose network; no internet-reachable port is opened and no human/admin auth is required because the SG never exposes the port.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Declare the status endpoint's access class and network binding explicitly: either (a) loopback-only (`127.0.0.1`) with a rationale that only same-host processes (land, observability.sh) consume it, or (b) nginx-proxied at a named path with TLS — and add the corresponding nginx location block and port binding to the plan's Files/AC.
findings.finding: The status endpoint's network binding and access posture are undeclared. The AC says 'bound so agents can reach it' but does not specify loopback-only vs. all-interfaces, whether it is proxied through nginx (adding TLS), or what access class it holds (public-with-rationale / authenticated / internal-only). The data served includes internal git SHAs (tested_shas), pipeline phase, and queue depth — operationally sensitive even if not cryptographically secret. An undeclared posture on a boundary-crossing surface is itself the T5c failure.
findings.location: AC: 'Read-only status endpoint' — 'bound so agents can reach it'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the AC: 'The status endpoint MUST bind to 127.0.0.1:<AUTOLANDER_STATUS_PORT> (loopback only); it is not proxied through nginx and is reachable only by same-host processes (land, observability.sh). Access class: internal-only (loopback). No auth required at this binding.' Add the port-binding line to docker-compose.yml (under the autolander service, using the 127.0.0.1:port:port form matching review-bot's pattern) to the Files list.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add explicit test coverage for feeders 2 and 3 to the Tests AC — e.g. a mock `docker inspect` output returning `unhealthy` asserts a metric datapoint, and a simulated `lander_down` event asserts a metric datapoint.
findings.finding: The Tests AC covers feeder 1 (AUTOLANDER_ERROR stdout marker) but specifies no test for feeder 2 (docker inspect health-state) or feeder 3 (lander_down/handback events) of the `autolander_errors` metric, leaving two of three feeders without a mapped verification step.
findings.location: AC: Tests + AC: Error metric (LOUD by default)
findings.suggested_fix: Extend the Tests AC: 'a mocked `docker inspect` returning `unhealthy` triggers a metric datapoint; a simulated `lander_down` event triggers a metric datapoint.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concurrency-safety specification for the status endpoint's access to `wipChain`: e.g., the HTTP handler reads an atomically-replaced snapshot dict (updated by the loop after each phase transition) rather than reading live mutable state directly.
findings.finding: The HTTP status endpoint exposes `wipChain` (mutable state owned and written by the main loop thread) to concurrent HTTP reader threads. No locking, copy-on-write, or atomic snapshot strategy is described, creating a TOCTOU race where the reader can observe a partially-updated chain.
findings.location: Files: `infra/autolander/loop.py` — 'Read-only HTTP status endpoint'; AC: 'current `wipChain` (change, `phase`, `tested_shas`, time-in-phase)'
findings.suggested_fix: Have the loop maintain a `status_snapshot` dict that is atomically replaced (a single Python reference assignment is GIL-safe) at each phase transition; the HTTP handler reads only `status_snapshot`, never the live `wipChain` object directly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a test case for the status endpoint when `wipChain` is empty/None (idle state), asserting the JSON shape remains valid and `heartbeat_age_s` is present.
findings.finding: The Tests AC covers the status endpoint only for the happy-path (documented JSON shape with heartbeat_age_s), but specifies no test for the empty or None wipChain case, which is a realistic startup or idle state that would produce a different JSON shape or error response.
findings.location: AC line — Tests bullet: 'the status endpoint returns the documented JSON shape (incl. heartbeat_age_s)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC test: 'when wipChain is empty, the endpoint returns {"wipChain": null, "autosubmit_waiting": 0, "heartbeat_age_s": <int>} (or equivalent sentinel), not a 500 or malformed JSON.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend the Tests AC to include a test for each of the three error-metric feeders: the AUTOLANDER_ERROR stdout path (already present), the docker-inspect unhealthy-state path, and the lander_down/handback path, each asserting a metric datapoint is emitted.
findings.finding: The Tests AC specifies only one of the three error-metric feeders ('a forced error produces an AUTOLANDER_ERROR marker + a metric datapoint'), leaving the docker-inspect unhealthy-state feeder and the lander_down/handback feeder without any stated test coverage.
findings.location: AC line — Tests bullet: 'a forced error produces an AUTOLANDER_ERROR marker + a metric datapoint'; AC line — Error metric AC specifying THREE feeders

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the HEALTHCHECK precondition for the `docker inspect --format '{{.State.Health.Status}}'` feeder: either confirm S5d (HEALTHCHECK ticket) ships before or together with S5c, or specify a fallback behavior (e.g., treat missing Health sub-object as 'not unhealthy' with an explicit shell guard) so the feeder does not silently fail.
findings.finding: The `docker inspect --format '{{.State.Health.Status}}'` feeder for autolander_errors silently fails when no HEALTHCHECK is configured on the autolander container: Docker returns `<no value>` for the Health sub-object in that case, not 'unhealthy'. The autolander container's HEALTHCHECK is in a sibling ticket (S5d), and this plan does not state the HEALTHCHECK precondition or a fallback for when Health is absent.
findings.location: AC: Error metric (LOUD by default) — 'the container `unhealthy` Docker health-state (via `docker inspect --format '{{.State.Health.Status}}'')'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a guard in observability.sh: `health=$(docker inspect --format '{{if .State.Health}}{{.State.Health.Status}}{{else}}none{{end}}' <container> 2>/dev/null)` and publish the metric only when `health = 'unhealthy'`. Also note in the plan that this feeder is inert until S5d's HEALTHCHECK is present.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a test file entry to the Files section (e.g. `tests/test_autolander_observability.py` — NEW or EDIT) covering the three test cases named in the Tests AC: endpoint JSON shape, forced-error marker+datapoint, and alarm-expression unit check.
findings.finding: The Tests AC specifies three concrete test deliverables (status-endpoint JSON shape including `heartbeat_age_s`; forced-error producing `AUTOLANDER_ERROR` marker + metric datapoint; time-in-phase alarm expression unit-check), but no test file is listed in the Files section. This leaves test work with no home file and no covered deliverable path.
findings.location: Files section (no test file listed) vs. Acceptance Criteria — Tests bullet
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Files: `tests/test_autolander_observability.py` — NEW (or EDIT if it exists): unit tests for (1) status endpoint JSON shape incl. `heartbeat_age_s`, (2) forced-error path emitting `AUTOLANDER_ERROR` marker and a metric datapoint, (3) time-in-phase alarm CloudFormation/Terraform expression correctness.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a measurement/derivation plan for the stuck-state alarm threshold (e.g., observe p95 phase-duration from real or shadow traffic before committing a value, or document it as `TBD: derive from first week of prod data` with a placeholder + review note in the TF file).
findings.finding: The stuck-state alarm threshold ('past a threshold') in monitoring_autolander.tf is asserted with no derivation or measurement plan. There is no stated baseline, no plan to observe real phase-duration distributions before picking a value, and no 'TBD: derive from data' note — making the threshold an arbitrary guess that may produce chronic false positives or miss real hangs.
findings.location: AC: Stuck-state alarm (high visibility) / infra/terraform/monitoring_autolander.tf
findings.suggested_fix: In monitoring_autolander.tf add a comment block citing the derivation basis (e.g., 'set to 2× the p95 observed phase duration from the first 7 days of prod data; revisit at 30 days'). Add a follow-up ticket to tighten the threshold after initial data is collected.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an ADR or CLAUDE.md entry capturing (a) the decision to embed an HTTP status endpoint in loop.py (with the Zuul/Tide prior-art rationale), and (b) the AUTOLANDER_ERROR stdout-marker contract (exact format, which consumers parse it, and what constitutes a breaking change).
findings.finding: The plan introduces two novel architectural patterns — an HTTP status endpoint embedded in loop.py and the AUTOLANDER_ERROR structured-stdout marker as a cross-component contract between loop.py and observability.sh — with no ADR, CLAUDE.md update, or design-doc capturing these decisions.
findings.location: Files: infra/autolander/loop.py, infra/scripts/observability.sh / AC: Read-only status endpoint, Error metric (LOUD by default)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Expose the stuck-state (and error-count) alarm thresholds as Terraform variables with sensible defaults, so they can be tuned per-environment without code changes.
findings.finding: The stuck-state alarm threshold (and any error-count alarm threshold) will be hardcoded values in monitoring_autolander.tf with no configurable variable, even though these thresholds are explicitly expected to evolve as the system matures and real phase-duration data accumulates.
findings.location: infra/terraform/monitoring_autolander.tf
findings.suggested_fix: In monitoring_autolander.tf: `variable "autolander_stuck_phase_threshold_minutes" { default = 30 }` and reference it in the alarm resource, with a comment noting the expected tuning lifecycle.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a timeout (e.g., `--cli-read-timeout 5 --cli-connect-timeout 3`) and at least one retry (e.g., `aws … || aws … || echo 'WARN: metric publish failed'`) to each PutMetricData call in observability.sh, and document that metric-publish failures are non-fatal but logged to stdout.
findings.finding: The observability.sh PutMetricData feeders (three sources) have no retry, backoff, or graceful-degradation strategy for AWS API unavailability. If the AWS endpoint is slow or throttling, the script will fail silently or block, and the error/TTL metrics will go dark without alerting anyone.
findings.location: infra/scripts/observability.sh — AC: Error metric (LOUD by default)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify deploy ordering (autolander before `land`) and document how `land` tolerates a temporarily absent status endpoint (e.g., endpoint absence = `lander_down`, or a grace period) so rollout and rollback are safe.
findings.finding: The plan does not specify deploy ordering between the autolander bot (which gains the status endpoint) and the `land` service (which binds `lander_down` to `heartbeat_age_s` on that endpoint). If `land` is deployed first or already depends on the endpoint, the window where the endpoint is absent is unspecified behavior.
findings.location: AC: Read-only status endpoint — '`heartbeat_age_s` (integer seconds since the last heartbeat write — the exact field `land`'s `lander_down` binds to)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a deploy note: 'Deploy autolander first; `land` must tolerate HTTP connection errors on the status endpoint as equivalent to `lander_down=true` so rollback does not wedge the land flow.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that `infra/autolander/loop.py` actually exists (created by S2a/S2b/S2c) with `wipChain`, `phase`, `tested_shas`, and `AUTOLANDER_ERROR` already present before treating this file as an EDIT target; if those siblings have not yet landed, add them as explicit `depends_on` prerequisites.
findings.finding: `infra/autolander/loop.py` is labeled EDIT in the Files section and multiple AC bullets assert it already contains `wipChain`, `phase`, `tested_shas`, time-in-phase tracking, and `AUTOLANDER_ERROR` emission — but the entire `infra/autolander/` directory does not exist in the repo. Grep for wipChain, AUTOLANDER_ERROR, tested_shas, heartbeat_age_s in the infra tree returns zero hits; `list_directory('infra/autolander')` returns ENOENT. All AC deliverables that say 'the loop writes…' or expose loop state rest on the false premise that this file already exists with the stated symbols.
findings.location: Files section: `infra/autolander/loop.py — EDIT`; AC: Read-only status endpoint, Error metric, Stuck-state alarm, Time-to-land metric
findings.suggested_fix: Add `depends_on: [S2a (89f9), S2b (a9e3), S2c (1ccf)]` to the ticket deps and change the Files entry to note that loop.py is created by those siblings; the precondition check in verify_commands should grep for `wipChain` and `AUTOLANDER_ERROR` in loop.py before proceeding.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate the `land`-side file(s) that consume the `heartbeat_age_s` field (or acknowledge the cross-ticket contract explicitly) so the endpoint address/field name is locked between this ticket and the S4 `land` implementation.
findings.finding: The `land` service — which the AC states 'binds `lander_down` to `heartbeat_age_s`' on the status endpoint this ticket introduces — is a consumer outside `infra/autolander/` and outside `infra/scripts/` that depends on the endpoint's address/port and field contract. No file path for `land`'s implementation is named anywhere in this plan, and no update to `land`'s configuration or code is listed in the file impact section.
findings.location: AC: Read-only status endpoint; Files section
findings.suggested_fix: Add a note under ## Files naming the `land` implementation file (e.g. `infra/land/client.py` or equivalent) with 'CONSUMER: reads `heartbeat_age_s` from the status endpoint; field name and port must match'; or add an explicit cross-ticket contract note stating the field name and endpoint address are a shared interface frozen by this ticket.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate the full `phase` value vocabulary (e.g., `idle | testing | submitting | rebasing | …`) in this plan's stuck-state alarm AC, or explicitly cross-reference the defining ticket, so the Terraform alarm expression has a complete schema to work from.
findings.finding: The `phase` enum value vocabulary is referenced but never defined in this plan. The stuck-state alarm AC says 'fires when a `wipChain` sits in any non-`idle` phase past a threshold,' but the complete set of valid `phase` values is never enumerated here or cross-referenced to a defining source. An agent writing the CloudWatch metric math expression or alarm condition in `monitoring_autolander.tf` must invent the phase vocabulary.
findings.location: AC: Stuck-state alarm (high visibility)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a parenthetical to the stuck-state alarm AC listing all valid phase enum values, e.g.: '…any non-`idle` phase (full set: `idle`, `testing`, `submitting`, `rebasing`)…' and reference the upstream ticket that owns the state machine.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
